claim: Goal shall be its own first-class struct type (not a Requirement facet) with typed anchor 'GOAL-'.
why: Atom of R-goal-as-target-state (type identity concern). In the Go port, Goal is defined as a dedicated struct in internal/ontology/process.go (fields: ID, Owner, TargetState, Lifecycle, Why, DeclOrder), distinct from the Requirement struct -- it is not a facet or field of Requirement. The canonical lifecycle is GoalLifecycle (internal/ontology/process.go: ACTIVE -> MET / ABANDONED). The typed-anchor discipline is enforced by check_typed_anchors_goal (internal/invariants/typed_anchors.go), which verifies every Goal.ID starts with 'GOAL-'. M19 resolved.
